steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the coating is suitable for "common applications" unlike other approaches that require special conditions, and that it reduces friction to nearly nonexistent levels — cutting both energy consumption and wear.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It is unlikely to reduce wear in mechanical systems that have multiple moving parts.: contradicts the passage, which explicitly links near-zero friction to reduced wear. ✗
  • Choice B — It is unlikely to be widely used because it can be applied only under special conditions.: the passage states the opposite — the coating works under common conditions, unlike other approaches. ✗
  • Choice C — It can likely be used to reduce energy consumption and wear in vehicle transmissions and other common mechanical systems.: matches both claims — near-zero friction addresses energy loss and wear, and the coating's broad applicability covers common systems. ✓
  • Choice D — It can likely improve the energy consumption of certain mechanical systems but not of vehicle transmissions.: the passage emphasizes broad suitability, not exclusions. ✗
That is the correct answer.
